description:
You are a junior log-analyst who has just received the web-server access log
located at

    /home/user/logs/webserver.log

Your lead wants one very small piece of automation that can be re-used later.
Create a **single, self-contained Bourne-shell script** called

    /home/user/analyze_errors.sh

that does the following when executed with no arguments:

1. Reads /home/user/logs/webserver.log (do **not** modify this source file).
2. Counts how many log lines contain the exact HTTP status code **404**.
   • “404” must be matched as a complete field, not as part of a longer number
     (e.g., “4040” must **not** be counted).
3. Writes (overwriting any existing file) exactly **one line** to

       /home/user/logs/404_report.log

   The required line format is:

       404 Error Count: <NUMBER>

   where <NUMBER> is the decimal count with no leading zeros and no extra
   whitespace.

4. Exits with a zero exit status if the output file is generated successfully;
   otherwise exits non-zero.

After you finish, execute your script once so that
/home/user/logs/404_report.log is present and contains the correct line.  
Nothing else should be written to stdout or to any other file.

The automated tests will verify BOTH of these things:

A) The existence of /home/user/analyze_errors.sh, that it is executable and
    produces the correct file when run.
B) The exact contents of /home/user/logs/404_report.log.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent starts):

1. Directory: /home/user/logs  (permissions 755)
2. File: /home/user/logs/webserver.log  (permissions 644)
   Exact contents:

127.0.0.1 - - [10/Oct/2023:13:55:36 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:01:02 +0000] "GET /not-found HTTP/1.1" 404 512
203.0.113.5 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:03:17 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 214
198.51.100.23 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:06:44 +0000] "GET /old-page HTTP/1.1" 404 198
192.0.2.77 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:09:58 +0000] "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.1" 200 543
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:12:11 +0000] "GET /robots.txt HTTP/1.1" 404 87
203.0.113.5 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:14:29 +0000] "GET /missing.jpg HTTP/1.1" 404 64
198.51.100.23 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:17:03 +0000] "GET /archive.zip HTTP/1.1" 200 3245
192.0.2.77 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:19:43 +0000] "HEAD /secret HTTP/1.1" 404 0
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Oct/2023:14:22:55 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024

Total 404 lines = 5  (lines #2, #4, #6, #7, #9)

Files the agent must create:

1. /home/user/analyze_errors.sh  (permissions 755)
   Must be a POSIX-compliant sh script implementing the requirements.

2. /home/user/logs/404_report.log  (permissions 644)
   Exact contents after agent runs the script:

404 Error Count: 5

No trailing spaces or newline after the single newline terminator.

The test harness will:
• check that analyze_errors.sh exists, is executable, and when executed
  produces exactly the above line in /home/user/logs/404_report.log.
• verify the script exits with status 0 on success.
